description: Verified nuclear facilities in the Middle East / wider theatre. Coordinates from open-source IAEA, NTI, and academic sources.

properties.name: Natanz Fuel Enrichment Plant
properties.notes: Primary uranium enrichment site; underground centrifuge halls

properties.name: Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant
properties.notes: Deeply buried underground enrichment facility near Qom

properties.name: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
properties.notes: Iran's first and only operational nuclear power reactor; Russian-built

properties.name: Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center
properties.notes: UF6 conversion plant; fuel fabrication; research reactors

properties.name: Arak IR-40 Heavy Water Reactor
properties.notes: Redesigned under JCPOA; heavy water production facility co-located

properties.name: Parchin Military Complex
properties.notes: Suspected high-explosive testing for nuclear weapon initiation

properties.name: Dimona Negev Nuclear Research Center
properties.notes: Israel's primary nuclear weapons facility; officially undeclared

properties.name: Soreq Nuclear Research Center
properties.notes: Research reactor; isotope production; civilian nuclear R&D

properties.name: Deir ez-Zor Reactor Site (Al-Kibar)
properties.notes: Suspected gas-cooled reactor; destroyed by Israeli airstrike Sep 2007

properties.name: Miniature Neutron Source Reactor (MNSR)
properties.notes: Chinese-supplied research reactor at Damascus; status uncertain post-civil war

properties.name: Barakah Nuclear Power Plant
properties.notes: Arab world's first operational nuclear power plant; 4 APR-1400 reactors

properties.name: King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology (KACARE) Research Reactor
properties.notes: Saudi Arabia pursuing civilian nuclear program; site under development

properties.name: Incirlik Air Base (NATO nuclear sharing)
properties.notes: Estimated 50 B61 tactical nuclear gravity bombs under NATO sharing agreement

properties.name: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant
properties.notes: Europe's largest NPP; under Russian military occupation since Mar 2022; IAEA monitoring

properties.name: Chernobyl Exclusion Zone
properties.notes: 1986 reactor disaster site; temporarily occupied by Russia Feb–Mar 2022
